assignments.comment: Extract length from Abmessungen (1st number), convert mm to cm

options.script: (() => { const normalized = replaceInText($("Abmessungen"), ",", "."); const nums = extractAllNumbersFromText(normalized); const val = nums.length > 0 ? parseFloat(nums[0]) : null; return val === null ? "" : (containsText($("Abmessungen"), "mm") ? String(val / 10) : String(val)); })()

assignments.comment: Extract width from Abmessungen (2nd number), convert mm to cm

options.script: (() => { const normalized = replaceInText($("Abmessungen"), ",", "."); const nums = extractAllNumbersFromText(normalized); const val = nums.length > 1 ? parseFloat(nums[1]) : null; return val === null ? "" : (containsText($("Abmessungen"), "mm") ? String(val / 10) : String(val)); })()

assignments.comment: Extract height from Abmessungen (3rd number), convert mm to cm

options.script: (() => { const normalized = replaceInText($("Abmessungen"), ",", "."); const nums = extractAllNumbersFromText(normalized); const val = nums.length > 2 ? parseFloat(nums[2]) : null; return val === null ? "" : (containsText($("Abmessungen"), "mm") ? String(val / 10) : String(val)); })()

objects.systemPrompt:
You are a precise data extraction assistant.
You extract structured attributes from a single source data record according to a list of target attributes.

You will receive a user message with two JSON blocks:
1. "Target attributes" — a JSON array describing what to extract.
2. "Source record" — a JSON object containing the raw data.

Each target attribute is an object with these fields:
- "key" (required): the property name to use in your output.
- "description" (required): what this attribute represents and what to look for in the source record.
- "fixedValues" (optional): a list of allowed values. If present, the output MUST be exactly one of these values, copied verbatim.
- "defaultValue" (optional): the value to use when the source record contains no usable information for this attribute.
- "mandatory" (optional, default false): when true, the attribute MUST appear in the output. If you cannot extract a value, fall back to "defaultValue" if given, otherwise use an empty string. Do not invent data.
- "pattern" (optional): a regular expression the output value must match. Reformat the extracted value if needed so it satisfies the pattern.

Output rules:
- Respond with a single flat JSON object — no markdown fences, no commentary, no wrapping array.
- Each property name in the output is a "key" from the target attributes.
- Map as many attributes as possible. Non-mandatory attributes with no source information may be omitted.
- Never fabricate values not supported by the source record. When in doubt, omit (or, for mandatory attributes, fall back as described above).
objects.name: Extract Details
objects.prompt:
Target attributes:
"""{{targetFormat}}"""

Source record:
"""{{sourceRecord}}"""

Always use source attribute EAN_Nr as the target attribute gtin. gtin is the identifier and needs to be present as much as possible

description.value: Global Trade Item Number. Must be a valid EAN-13 (13 digits, correct check digit).
Screen or display diagonal size in inches. Numeric decimal (e.g. 15.60). Null for non-display products.
Full processor or SoC model designation as published by the manufacturer (e.g. 'Intel Core i7-13700H', 'Apple M3', 'Snapdragon 8 Gen 3').
RAM or unified memory capacity in gigabytes. Integer (e.g. 16). For unified memory architectures use total unified memory.
Primary internal storage capacity in gigabytes. Integer (e.g. 512). Use base storage SKU value.
Battery capacity in milliampere-hours. Integer. If only watt-hour figure available, convert at nominal 3.7V (Wh × 1000 / 3.7).
Manufacturer-stated typical battery life in hours under standard use conditions. Numeric decimal (e.g. 13.00).
Highest WiFi standard supported. Must match one of the defined values exactly.
Bluetooth specification version. Must match one of the defined values.
Pre-installed or native operating system. Must match one of the defined values. Use 'Other' if none apply.
Whether the device supports 5G mobile connectivity. Boolean.
Whether the device has Near Field Communication (NFC) support. Boolean.
